concerns.text: The plan assumes training a 1.1B parameter model (TinyLlama) on a 2-CPU GitHub Actions runner within 2 hours. This is computationally infeasible. Even with batch size 1, a single forward/backward pass for 1.1B parameters on CPU takes seconds; processing 100k tokens would require hours, likely exceeding the 2-hour budget and the 7GB RAM limit due to activation memory for the recursive module. The dataset size (100k tokens) is appropriate for the constraint, but the model size is not.

The plan cites 'TinyLlama-1.1B' as the base model. Given the 7GB RAM constraint and the addition of a recursive self-attention module (which increases memory footprint), this model will likely cause Out-Of-Memory (OOM) errors during the forward pass, especially with recursion depth 2. The plan acknowledges this risk but treats it as a 'run failure' rather than a feasibility issue. A smaller model (e.g., <300M parameters) is required to fit the compute budget.

The plan proposes using the 'arXiv' subset of The Pile for training. While the URL is reachable, the dataset is raw text. The proposed 'confidence-prediction loss' relies on a 'self-consistency proxy' (majority vote of 3 generations) as a pseudo-label. This requires generating 3 full sequences per training token to create the label, effectively tripling the inference cost during training. Combined with the 1.1B model size and CPU constraints, this makes the training loop computationally impossible within the 2-hour window.
